rules.message: A tainted-source access (request body/query/params/headers, `searchParams`, `process.argv`) and a dangerous sink (`eval`, `new Function`, `exec`/`spawn`, raw SQL, `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, `.innerHTML`) both appear in the same function — this is a coarse v1 check (source+sink co-occurrence, not real dataflow), so confirm the value is actually tainted here. For a SQL sink, parameterize the query (prepared/parameterized statement API) — never escape or interpolate the tainted value into the SQL string. For a shell/process-exec sink, use `execFile`/`spawn` with an args array, not a shell-string `exec()` with string concatenation. For a DOM/HTML sink, use `textContent` (not `innerHTML`) for plain text, or a vetted HTML-sanitizer library if HTML must be inserted — never raw `innerHTML` with tainted input. Suppress a vetted case with `// taint-ok`.
